heading: Deficiency judgment
label: 3768 Deficiency judgment
notes: CodificationSection is based on section 819 of title VIII of S. 2281, One Hundred Third Congress, as reported , which was enacted into law by .July 13, 1994Pub. L. 103–327

children.heading: Referral to Attorney General
children.label: 1 Referral to Attorney General
children.text: If after deducting the payments provided for in , the price at which the security property is sold at a foreclosure sale is less than the unpaid balance of the debt secured by the security property, resulting in a deficiency, the Secretary may refer the matter to the Attorney General who may commence an action or actions against any or all debtors to recover the deficiency, unless such an action is specifically prohibited by the mortgage.section 3762 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any amount authorized by ; andsection 3011 of title 28

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: b Limitation
children.text: Any action commenced to recover a deficiency under this section must be brought not later than 6 years after the date of the last sale of the security property.
